headline: Pampers Amore Premium Launch Tests Procter And Gamble Pricing Power
summary: Procter & Gamble (NYSE:PG) has introduced Pampers AMORE, presented as its most premium and absorbent diaper. The new line features five-layer leak protection and dermatologist-recognized skin safety, with a focus on ingredient transparency. Pampers AMORE is the first new diaper range from Pampers since 2018, targeting parents focused on comfort and sensitive skin care. For NYSE:PG, Pampers sits at the core of its baby care portfolio, a category where parents often look for both performance...

headline: VIG Returned 223% Over 10 Years and Still Costs Almost Nothing
summary: A 1.5% yield sounds unimpressive until you realize the dividend check doubles roughly every seven years. That is the premise behind the Vanguard Dividend Appreciation ETF (NYSEARCA:VIG): skip the high-yield traps and own companies that raise their dividends year after year, compounding income alongside capital appreciation. What VIG Is Actually Trying to Do VIG tracks ... VIG Returned 223% Over 10 Years and Still Costs Almost Nothing

headline: 4 Consumer Favorites Look Oversold Right Now: Disney, McDonald’s and More
summary: Four household names are now flashing oversold signals. McDonald’s (NYSE: MCD), Microsoft (NASDAQ: MSFT), Procter & Gamble (NYSE: PG), and Walt Disney (NYSE: DIS) have each seen their RSI(14) readings collapse below 30 as of March 24, 2026, signaling extreme selling pressure. The underlying businesses are performing well, yet the stocks have been sold down below ... 4 Consumer Favorites Look Oversold Right Now: Disney, McDonald’s and More

headline: SCHD Just Made Big Changes. Is This Dividend Growth ETF Still a Buy?
summary: The Schwab U.S. Dividend Equity ETF (NYSEARCA:SCHD) stands as one of the most popular dividend-growth vehicles on the market. Since its inception on October 20, 2011, the fund has delivered a cumulative total return of 478%, equating to a 13.3% annualized average. With a current yield of 3.3%, SCHD has turned a hypothetical $10,000 investment ... SCHD Just Made Big Changes. Is This Dividend Growth ETF Still a Buy?
